title: rubus × idaeiformis
intro: Rubus × idaeiformis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Japanese wineberry, is a hybrid of the species Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus, characterized by its edible, aggregate fruits and thorny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The health benefits of Rubus × idaeiformis include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or by mixing the plant's extracts with a solvent such as ethanol.
